evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Applying testing, documentation, and version control best practices
evidence.domain_risk: Defining data contracts, metrics, and semantic models across business domains
evidence.data_team_maturity: Developing and maintaining dbt models transforming raw data into analytics-ready datasets in Snowflake
evidence.jd_authorship: Developing and maintaining dbt models transforming raw data into analytics-ready datasets in Snowflake
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Contributing to data platform architecture including bronze/silver/gold layers, CI/CD pipelines, data governance, and monitoring
evidence.autonomy_level: Defining data contracts, metrics, and semantic models across business domains
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Developing and maintaining dbt models frames the work around extending and sustaining existing data infrastructure rather than building it from scratch, supporting fix_scale.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The JD covers dbt, Snowflake, data contracts, and platform architecture in depth but never mentions AI tools, AI-assisted coding, or AI-consuming infrastructure.
evidence.testing_framing: Applying testing, documentation, and version control best practices
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: The verb 'Applying' paired with explicit testing practice as a core responsibility indicates the candidate personally owns quality assurance rather than merely working alongside a listed tool.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Contributing to data platform architecture including bronze/silver/gold layers, CI/CD pipelines, data governance, and monitoring
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Data governance and monitoring reflect a concern with preventing pipeline incidents and schema drift, a secondary operational-reliability concern rather than compliance or regulatory framing.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: JD emphasizes testing, documentation, data contracts, governance, and monitoring with zero velocity language present.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: No financial reporting, compliance, or regulatory language despite technical rigor; standard analytics operations without explicit high-risk domain implications.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools (dbt, Snowflake) appear in responsibilities with bronze/silver/gold architecture, indicating established function being scaled rather than greenfield.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific technical context about named tools, their precise application (dbt transformation workflows), and technology stack indicates someone with hands-on experience writing this.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Primary emphasis on data platform infrastructure, governance tooling, and serving the data function rather than commercial, product, or finance teams.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Strategic responsibility for defining data contracts and semantic models combined with execution responsibilities like developing/maintaining dbt models and contributing to architecture.
